spec_match.rationale: The analyzer now fetches same-origin probe resources and reads the resulting PerformanceResourceTiming.contentType values, matching the spec getter that returns the fetch resource-info content type.

interactive.rationale: Probe-set select, fallback-sample checkbox, analyze/clear buttons, and dynamic MIME filters all change the fetched resources, stats, chart buckets, table rows, and audit output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still delivers the feature-index promise: it fetches eight resource types, groups by PerformanceResourceTiming.contentType, compares transfer and decoded sizes, and flags unexpected types.

edge_cases.rationale: The edge probe set covers mislabelled script responses and a missing Content-Type response; fallback samples cover cached, opaque, and mislabelled rows.

references.rationale: The references now cite the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5156068351541248 link and the Resource Timing spec.

design_system.rationale: Inline styles use CSS variables, button background overrides include colors, and touched uppercase headings use zero letter spacing.

summary: Re-tested with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome 148 at /v148/content-type-in-resource-timing/mime-type-performance-analyzer/?verify=after-filter-state. Before the fix, Analyze resources rendered mock rows and issued zero same-origin probe fetches, and the page linked ChromeStatus 5113591087226880. After the fix, the core set fetched 8 real same-origin probe resources and displayed PerformanceResourceTiming.contentType values; the edge set fetched mislabelled HTML/plain script responses plus a no-Content-Type response; fallback samples were explicitly labelled; MIME filters and Clear data worked; console had no errors. The feature conformance route reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL, including a new same-origin CSS contentType assertion.
